<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Repair Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2747)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A repair toolkit allows you to perform simple repairs while traveling. It contains a portable anvil, tongs, woodworking tools, a whetstone, and oils for conditioning leather and wood. You can use a repair toolkit to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) items using the [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) skill. You can draw and replace a worn repair toolkit as part of the action that uses it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thieves' Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 292

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** -1

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You need a thieves' toolkit to [Pick Locks](/Actions.aspx?ID=70) or [Disable Devices](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) (of some types) using the [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) skill. If your thieves' toolkit is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), you can repair it by replacing the lock picks with replacement picks appropriate to your toolkit; this doesn't require using the [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) action. If you wear your thieves' toolkit, you can draw and replace it as part of the action that uses it.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Electric Eelskin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2805)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 230

**Price** 950 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Shining, slippery eelskin covers the plates of this _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) [greater slick](/Equipment.aspx?ID=289) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_. The armor gives you the ability to breathe water and grants you a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks you attempt in the water. 

**Activate—Unleash Charge** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast a 2nd-rank [_thunderstrike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1721) with a DC of 29.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of _thunderstrike_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Electric Eelskin

    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tideplate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2810)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 231

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Rippling water motifs decorate this simple suit of _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_. The plate has been altered for underwater use, so it's check penalty doesn't apply to Acrobatics or Athletics checks in water or similar liquids. While wearing the armor, you gain a +2 item bonus to Athletics checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39), and you can breathe underwater. 

**Activate—Ride the Waves** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The ripples on the armor begin to undulate, matching the movement of any nearby water. The armor casts [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) on you. 

**Activate—Submerge** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127))
 **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You merge with the water for 10 minutes. While merged, you can't move, you can see through the water if it's clear enough, and you can hear what's going on outside of the water. Water typically can't take damage, but if the water you're merged in is subject to electricity damage or an ability or effect that destroys or dries water, you're expelled from the water and take 10d6 damage. [_Control water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=51) expels you without dealing damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tideplate

<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />

Etchings of powerful bears decorate these brass bracers. You gain a +3 item bonus to Athletics checks and a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to lift a heavy object, [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79), and [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34). When you invest the bracers, you either increase your Strength modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. 

**Activate—Bear Hug** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** Attempt to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) a creature. If you succeed, you crush the creature in your grasp, dealing bludgeoning damage to it equal to your Strength modifier. If you critically succeeded, the damage is equal to double your Strength modifier and the creature [suffocates](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439) as long as it remains grabbed or restrained by you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bracers of Strength

This thin cloak is surprisingly light, as if clouds or the very wind were woven together to make the garment. The cloak grants you a +3 item bonus to Acrobatics checks. When you invest the cloak, you either increase your Dexterity modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate—Ride the Wind** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You tug on the cloak, wrapping yourself in the power of wind. You gain a fly Speed of 30 feet for 1 hour. While wrapped in the cloak, you become translucent, causing you to become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) for the duration.

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Necklace of Allure](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3012)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=532" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 271

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Several sapphires are set along the length of this brilliant silver necklace. The necklace features an intricately carved pendant in the shape of a wolf's head. You gain a +2 item bonus to Deception and Diplomacy checks. When you invest the necklace, you either increase your Charisma modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. 

**Activate—Win Them Over** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast a 4th-rank [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) spell (DC 38).

</column>

    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alacritous Horsehoes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3013)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Companion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=558" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=676" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 272

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn horseshoes

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you affix these simple iron horseshoes to the hooves of an ordinary horse or a quadrupedal animal companion and the animal companion invests them, that creature gains a +5-foot item bonus to its land Speed and a +2 circumstance bonus to Athletics checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37). In addition, when it [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), it can move 5 feet farther if jumping horizontally or 3 feet higher if jumping vertically.

    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Marvelous Medicines](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3027)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 275

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [healer's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25) contains a seemingly endless supply of bandages, herbs, and healing items of impeccable quality, granting you a +2 item bonus to Medicine checks. If you use the _marvelous medicines_ when you [Treat a Poison](/Actions.aspx?ID=56) or [Treat a Disease](/Actions.aspx?ID=55), before you roll your check, the medicines attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the poison or disease you're treating, with a counteract rank of 5 and a counteract modifier of +21. This is a healing effect. The medicines can't be used to treat the same affliction for that patient again.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Possibility Tome](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3029)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An array of semiprecious stones is set into the ornate silver and beaten copper cover of this thick and weighty tome. If you open the book before it's been activated, its vellum pages are blank and pristine, but once activated, words dance and swim onto the pages before your eyes. 

**Activate—Skim** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** As you flip through the book, you think about a broad topic you want to know more about. Choose one skill: Arcana, Crafting, Medicine, Nature, Occultism, Religion, Society, or a single subcategory of Lore. The book's pages fill with information about that skill, though only you can see the information. While the pages are full, you can spend an Interact action perusing the book just before attempting a check to Recall Knowledge with the chosen skill.     right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Skeleton Key](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3030)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 276

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A grinning skull tops the bow of this macabre key. This key can be used in place of a thieves' toolkit when attempting to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70), and it grants a +1 item bonus to the Thievery check. If the skeleton key becomes broken due to a critical failure on the check, it works as a normal thieves' toolkit and loses its benefits until repaired. 

**Activate—Loosen Lock** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The key casts [_knock_](/Spells.aspx?ID=168) on the lock you're trying to pick.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Accolade Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3054)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Although not all wizards have gone through formal training, it's become tradition to enchant robes representing the arduous training required and festoon them with honors one has earned. Typically, an _accolade robe_ is styled after a single wizard school, with appropriate colors and symbols. Wearing these robes grants a +2 item bonus to Arcana checks.

 The pockets of the robe tie to an extradimensional space that can hold 1 Bulk of items, none of which can have greater than light Bulk. The items must be related to spellcasting and academics—spellbooks, scrolls, wands, scholarly journals, and other academic supplies the GM allows. These items do not count against your Bulk limit. You can Interact to retrieve or stow items normally. 

**Activate—Review** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You retrieve an item of your choice from the robe's storage, then Recall Knowledge. 

**Activate—Extra Credit** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a school spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a wizard of the associated school.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</row>

</column>

</traits>

</row>

</column>

---

Typically, an _accolade robe_ is styled after a single wizard school, with appropriate colors and symbols. Wearing these robes grants a +2 item bonus to Arcana checks.

 The pockets of the robe tie to an extradimensional space that can hold 1 Bulk of items, none of which can have greater than light Bulk. The items must be related to spellcasting and academics—spellbooks, scrolls, wands, scholarly journals, and other academic supplies the GM allows. These items do not count against your Bulk limit. You can Interact to retrieve or stow items normally. 

**Activate—Review** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You retrieve an item of your choice from the robe's storage, then Recall Knowledge. 

**Activate—Extra Credit** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a school spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a wizard of the associated school.

summary_markdown: The bonus to Arcana checks increases to +3. You can activate the robe’s Review once per minute as a free action.

item_bonus_note: Bonus corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone.
markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times.
When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again.
There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) Aeon Stone (Amplifying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _amplifying aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone. **Activate—Amplify** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell's rank is 1 higher (maximum 10th rank) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Consumed) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9 gp --- A _consumed aeon stone_ is a dull lump that has lost its magical properties. It still rotates your head like any other aeon stone and can thus serve as a stylish, hand-free option for various spells that target an object. This _aeon stone_ has no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Delaying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this smooth pink stone automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _consumed aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones.
The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Envisioning) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 425 gp --- This clear cube allows you to communicate with a limited form of telepathy to a range of 100 feet. The messages are transmitted as a simple image each round. These images are the quality of a hasty or childish sketch but can be roughly understood by creatures regardless of language. This provides no special ability to respond to your images.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Peering) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,500 gp --- This faintly colored prism catches and transforms light. If the _aeon stone_ is in an area of bright light, shades of purple light provide dim light in a 30-foot radius. In that radius, magical items and effects are tinged with purple, the depth of the color revealing their [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank.
The GM also secretly rolls a counteract check against any darkness or illusion effect the light touches. It has a counteract modifier of +25. If the light fails to counteract an effect, it won't try again for 24 hours.
Inside a _wayfinder_, the aeon stone cannot catch light. However, its resonant power lets you activate or deactivate the aeon stone's light emitting from the _wayfinder_ as an Interact action. Aeon Stone (Nourishing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 325 gp --- After you've invested and worn this transparent stone for a week continuously, you no longer need to eat or drink. This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Amplifying)

This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Amplifying)

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times.
When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again.
There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) Aeon Stone (Amplifying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _amplifying aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone. **Activate—Amplify** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell's rank is 1 higher (maximum 10th rank) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Consumed) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9 gp --- A _consumed aeon stone_ is a dull lump that has lost its magical properties. It still rotates your head like any other aeon stone and can thus serve as a stylish, hand-free option for various spells that target an object. This _aeon stone_ has no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Delaying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this smooth pink stone automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _consumed aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones.
The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Envisioning) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 425 gp --- This clear cube allows you to communicate with a limited form of telepathy to a range of 100 feet. The messages are transmitted as a simple image each round. These images are the quality of a hasty or childish sketch but can be roughly understood by creatures regardless of language. This provides no special ability to respond to your images.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Peering) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,500 gp --- This faintly colored prism catches and transforms light. If the _aeon stone_ is in an area of bright light, shades of purple light provide dim light in a 30-foot radius. In that radius, magical items and effects are tinged with purple, the depth of the color revealing their [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank.
The GM also secretly rolls a counteract check against any darkness or illusion effect the light touches. It has a counteract modifier of +25. If the light fails to counteract an effect, it won't try again for 24 hours.
Inside a _wayfinder_, the aeon stone cannot catch light. However, its resonant power lets you activate or deactivate the aeon stone's light emitting from the _wayfinder_ as an Interact action. Aeon Stone (Nourishing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 325 gp --- After you've invested and worn this transparent stone for a week continuously, you no longer need to eat or drink. This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Usage** worn --- Over millennia, these mysterious, intricately cut gemstones have been hoarded by mystics and fanatics hoping to discover their secrets. Despite their myriad forms and functions, these stones are purportedly all fragments of crystal tools used by otherworldly entities to construct the universe in primeval times.
When you invest one of these precisely shaped crystals, the stone orbits your head instead of being worn on your body. You can stow an _aeon stone_ with an Interact action, and an orbiting stone can be snatched out of the air with a successful Disarm action against you. A stowed or removed stone remains invested, but its effects are suppressed until you return it to orbit your head again.
There are various types of aeon stones, each with a different appearance and magical effect. Each aeon stone also gains a resonant power when slotted into a special magical item called a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) Aeon Stone (Amplifying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,750 gp --- An _amplifying aeon stone_ must be activated to provide a benefit. The resonant power grants you a +2 item bonus to Arcana, Nature, Occultism, or Religion checks—whichever corresponds to the tradition of the last spell you enhanced with this aeon stone. **Activate—Amplify** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Effect** If your next action is to Cast a Spell, that spell's rank is 1 higher (maximum 10th rank) for the purposes of counteracting and being counteracted. Aeon Stone (Consumed) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9 gp --- A _consumed aeon stone_ is a dull lump that has lost its magical properties. It still rotates your head like any other aeon stone and can thus serve as a stylish, hand-free option for various spells that target an object. This _aeon stone_ has no resonant power. Aeon Stone (Delaying) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 350 gp --- When you would die from the dying condition (typically at dying 4), this smooth pink stone automatically activates and reduces your dying value to 1 less than would normally kill you (typically to dying 3). The stone then permanently turns into a _consumed aeon stone_. You can benefit from this ability only once per day, even if you have multiple such stones.
The resonant power allows you to cast 1st-rank [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) as a divine innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Envisioning) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 425 gp --- This clear cube allows you to communicate with a limited form of telepathy to a range of 100 feet. The messages are transmitted as a simple image each round. These images are the quality of a hasty or childish sketch but can be roughly understood by creatures regardless of language. This provides no special ability to respond to your images.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_translate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) as an occult innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Peering) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 9,500 gp --- This faintly colored prism catches and transforms light. If the _aeon stone_ is in an area of bright light, shades of purple light provide dim light in a 30-foot radius. In that radius, magical items and effects are tinged with purple, the depth of the color revealing their [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) rank.
The GM also secretly rolls a counteract check against any darkness or illusion effect the light touches. It has a counteract modifier of +25. If the light fails to counteract an effect, it won't try again for 24 hours.
Inside a _wayfinder_, the aeon stone cannot catch light. However, its resonant power lets you activate or deactivate the aeon stone's light emitting from the _wayfinder_ as an Interact action. Aeon Stone (Nourishing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 325 gp --- After you've invested and worn this transparent stone for a week continuously, you no longer need to eat or drink. This time resets if you remove the _aeon stone_ or it's invested by someone else. The resonant power allows you to cast [_air bubble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=5) as a primal innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Preserving) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 150 gp --- This red crystalline star covers you in a faint aura when you are subject to lingering wounds. You gain resistance 3 to [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). At the end of any turn where the persistent damage can't overcome this resistance, end that condition.
The resonant power allows you to cast [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) as a primal innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Smoothing) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 310 gp --- This spherical piece of silvery stone helps you ignore minor issues. You can ignore status penalties to skill checks from [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) conditions as long as the value of that condition is 1. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=142) as an occult innate cantrip. Aeon Stone (Sprouting) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 284 **Price** 220 gp --- Encouraging the natural growth of life, this green ovoid can often be found surrounded by new seedlings. Its resonant power allows you to cast [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713) as a primal innate cantrip. **Activate—Flow of Life** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are healed by a vitality efect or took void damage; **Effect** You gain 8 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Aeon Stone (Formulating) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 81 **Price** 75 gp --- This four-sided prism is imbued with knowledge for the construction of several objects. The stone contains the knowledge of all formulas found in a basic crafter’s book. While you have the stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you have the full knowledge of the stone’s formulas. This counts as having the formula for the purposes of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. While you have this knowledge, you can write the formulas down for later use, such as if you lose access to the stone. You also gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) while the stone is invested. The resonant power allows you to cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Repairing) **Source** [Pathfinder #213: Thirst for Blood](/Sources.aspx?ID=257) pg. 82 **Price** 75 gp --- This translucent, spherical stone’s center contains a multitude of sparkling, colored flecks. While you have this stone [invested](/Traits.aspx?ID=637), you gain a +1 item bonus to skill checks made to Craft. Additionally, when Repairing an item, you restore an additional 5 Hit Points to the item (10 Hit Points on a critical success). The resonant power allows you to cast [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) as an arcane innate spell once per day. Aeon Stone (Crescent) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 225 gp --- Once holy relics, the creation of _crescent aeon stones_ was inspired by [Acavna](/Deities.aspx?ID=402), goddess of the moon and protection. The _crescent aeon stone_ continually sheds dim light in a 5-foot radius. The resonant power enables you to cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) innate cantrip. **Activate—Moonbeam** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crescent aeon stone fires a blast of silvery moonlight in a 100-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 4d12 spirit damage to all creatures in the area (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). This is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) damage for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like. Aeon Stone (Vital amplification) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 330 gp --- A _vital amplification aeon stone_ improves the flow of vital energy through your body, speeding the healing process and safeguarding your body from life-draining effects. Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain an additional 1 Hit Point for each 10 Hit Points regained (minimum 1 additional Hit Point). The resonant power grants you [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to void damage. Aeon Stone (Flickering) **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 1,200 gp --- A _flickering aeon stone_ contains a drop of orichalcum at its center. It remains slightly out of phase with reality, giving it a translucent appearance. **Activate—Flicker** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ draws you slightly out of sync with the flow of time, causing you to flicker in and out of existence. You become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) for 1 minute, but you can’t use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). **Activate—Enter Stasis** ; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _flickering aeon stone_ pulls you from the flow of time completely, placing you in temporary stasis while you heal, then returning you to reality at the moment you left. You regain 2d10+8 Hit Points. If you have the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition, the value of each of these conditions is reduced by 1. The resonant power grants you a +1 circumstance bonus to initiative rolls.
name: Aeon Stone (Repairing)

[Armbands of Athleticism](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3057)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 285

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armbands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Skilled awl work has imprinted images of a muscled weightlifter into these tiered leather bands, which grant you enhanced stamina and skill when performing athletic exercises. While fastened to your upper arms, the armbands give you a +2 item bonus to Athletics checks. In addition, whenever you use an action to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) and you succeed at the Athletics check, add a +5-foot item bonus to the distance you move.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

pfs="Standard"
>
Armbands of Athleticism
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 645 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Armbands of Athleticism (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 285, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 605

**Price** 13,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to Athletics checks is +3, and the bonus to a successful check to Climb or Swim is +10 feet.

</title>

<traits>

---

The springy soles of these sturdy leather boots cushion your feet and make each step lighter. These boots give you a +5- foot item bonus to your Speed and a +2 item bonus to Athletics checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37). In addition, when you use the Leap action, you can move 5 feet further if jumping horizontally or 3 feet higher if jumping vertically.

**Activate—Jangling Dash** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain a +10-foot status bonus to Speed for 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Charlatan's Cape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3063)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286

**Price** 980 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage**  worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bright red-and-gold cape is often interlaced with glittery threads and serves as a distraction. While wearing the cape, you gain a +2 item bonus to Deception checks. 

**Activate—Puff of Smoke** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724). The space you leave and the one you appear in are filled with puffs of smoke that make anyone within concealed until they leave the smoke or the end of your next turn, at which point the smoke dissipates. Strong winds immediately disperse the smoke.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Charlatan's Cape

    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Charlatan's Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3064)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Tiny silver hooks decorate these fine silk gloves. They grant a +1 item bonus to Thievery and allow you to cast [_telekinetic hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) as an innate occult spell. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Charlatan's Gloves
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Charlatan's Gloves (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 286

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The gloves' bonus increases to +2. If you are also wearing a _[charlatan's cape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3063)_, whenever you would move an object using [_telekinetic hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), you may instead have it disappear in a puff of smoke and reappear hovering in a space adjacent to yours. This is a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effect. The item hovers until the end of your turn or until retrieved with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action.
name: Charlatan's Gloves

    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Choker of Elocution](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3066)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 287

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn collar

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This platinum choker bears characters from a language's alphabet, and it gives knowledge of that language and the associated culture's customs. You gain a +1 item bonus to Society checks and the ability to understand, speak, and write the chosen language. Your excellent elocution reduces the DC of the flat check to perform an auditory action while deafened from 5 to 3.  

**Craft Requirements** You know the language or languages the choker grants.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

**Price** 200 gp

</column>

right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"

Choker of Elocution (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 287, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 608

**Price** 850 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2. The choker bears characters from three languages and grants fluency in all three.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clandestine Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3067)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 287

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you pull up the hood of this nondescript gray cloak (an Interact action), you become drab and uninteresting, gaining a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks and to Deception checks to Impersonate a forgettable background character, such as a servant, but also taking a –1 item penalty to Diplomacy and Intimidation checks. 

**Activate—Cloak Identity** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pull the cloak's hood up and gain the benefits of [_veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739) for 1 hour or until you pull the hood back down, whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clandestine Cloak
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 287, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 608

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clandestine Cloak (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 287, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 608

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and when you activate the cloak, you gain the benefits of 5th-rank [_veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739) for 8 hours.
name: Clandestine Cloak

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cloak of Illusions](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3069)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 287

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This cloak flows, covering and concealing the wearer's body. The cloak allows you to cast [_figment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528) as an occult innate cantrip. Although naturally a dull gray, while invested the cloak picks up colors and patterns from its surroundings, granting a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks. 

**Activate—Draw Hood** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw the hood up and gain the effects of [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), with the spell's normal duration or until you pull the hood back down, whichever comes first.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crafter's Eyepiece](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3072)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rugged metal eyepiece etched with square patterns is designed to be worn over a single eye. Twisting the lens reveals a faint three-dimensional outline of an item you plan to build or repair, with helpful labels on the component parts. You gain a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks. When you Repair an item, increase the Hit Points restored to 10 + 10 per proficiency rank on a success or 15 + 15 per proficiency rank on a critical success.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crafter's Eyepiece
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Crafter's Eyepiece (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The eyepiece grants a +2 item bonus and can be activated. 

**Activate—Prototype** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You calibrate the eyepiece to have it cast a 5th-rank [_creation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477) spell over the course of 1 minute to construct a temporary item.
name: Crafter's Eyepiece

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288 **Usage** worn headwear --- An elegant accoutrement for a witch who has come into the higher echelons of power, a _crown of witchcraft_ typically looks like a garland of flowering twigs, a jeweled circlet, or a tall hat of fine fabric. You gain a +1 item bonus to Intimidation checks, and if you're a witch, you gain a +2 item bonus to your patron skill.
If you have a familiar, you can attach a small portion of the crown's material to your familiar, such as a strip of fabric from a hat tied around its tail or a sprig of natural material linked to its collar; the familiar doesn't need to invest the item itself. If you do this, your familiar gains the [tough pet](/Familiars.aspx?ID=37) ability as long as the crown is invested by you. **Activate—Defiant Hex** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a witch [hex](/Traits.aspx?ID=324) spell. If you don't spend this point by the end of this turn, it is lost. **Craft Requirements** You are a witch. Crown of Witchcraft **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288 **Price** 1,000 gp Crown of Witchcraft (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288 **Price** 21,000 gp --- The bonus to Intimidation checks is +2 and the bonus to your patron skill is +3.
name: Crown of Witchcraft

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288 **Usage** worn shoes **Bulk** L --- These brightly colored, soft-soled boots motivate you to perform risky stunts and grant you the agility to succeed. The boots grant you a +2 item bonus to Acrobatics checks and a +1 circumstance bonus to checks to Tumble Through an enemy's space.
The boots can grip solid surfaces and help you avoid a fall, allowing you to use the Grab an Edge reaction even if your hands aren't free. You treat falls as 10 feet shorter or, if you have the Cat Fall feat, treat your proficiency rank in Acrobatics as one degree better to determine the benefits of that feat. If you have Cat Fall and are already legendary in Acrobatics, you can choose the speed of your fall, from 60 feet per round up to normal falling speed. Daredevil Boots **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609 **Price** 900 gp Daredevil Boots (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 609 **Price** 14,000 gp --- The bonus to Acrobatics checks is +3, and the bonus to Tumble Through is +2. The boots can be activated. **Activate—Daredevil Impulse** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The boots cast [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) on you.
name: Daredevil Boots

    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Devoted Vestments](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3077)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 288

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These vestments are made of panels showing various scenes from the legends of a particular deity. It serves as a religious symbol of that deity, and you gain a +2 item bonus to Religion checks. When you cast [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) or [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), healing granted to followers of that deity is increased by the rank of the spell. 

**Activate—Domain Devotion** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a cleric domain spell for a domain belonging to the deity the vestments are dedicated to. If you don't spend this point by the end of this turn, it is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a cleric who worships the deity tied to the vestments.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Devoted Vestments

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When displayed prominently, this brass badge makes creatures find you more agreeable. You gain a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks. 

**Activate—Diplomat's Bearing** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Attempt a DC 20 check to Recall Knowledge about people of a human ethnicity, a non- human ancestry, or some other type of creature. (The GM determines what your options are.) If you succeed, the badge's bonus increases to +2 for Diplomacy checks with creatures of that group for the rest of the day.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Diplomat's Badge

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289 **Price** 15,000 gp **Usage** worn eyepiece --- When tied over your eyes, this ragged strip of black linen gives you darkvision and a +3 item bonus to Intimidation checks. You can see through the blindfold, but only using darkvision.
The first time a particular creature sees you in a day, it must succeed at a DC 37 Will save or be [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 1. This is an [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect, and your allies become immune to it after a week. **Activate—Visions of Terror** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You damage a creature with a Strike; **Effect** Your target is gripped by intense fear. This has the effect of a DC 37 [_vision of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) spell. The creature is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
name: Dread Blindfold

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Entertainer's Cincture](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3082)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The designs adorning these lush sashes often imitate the decor of famous opera houses, theaters, and museums. When you invest this item, choose Deception, Diplomacy, Intimidation, or Performance; you gain a +2 item bonus to that skill. 

**Activate—Encore** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a bard composition spell. If you don't spend this point by the end of this turn, it is lost. 

**Activate—Transcribe** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You tuck a small roll of paper into the cincture. For the next 10 minutes or until you Dismiss the activation, any performance you make is recorded on the paper, and the paper expands as necessary to accommodate it. Depending on the type of performance, this might take the form of sheet music, a transcript, or a diagram of dance moves.

**Craft Requirements** You are a bard.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Entertainer's Cincture](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3082)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 289

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The designs adorning these lush sashes often imitate the decor of famous opera houses, theaters, and museums. When you invest this item, choose Deception, Diplomacy, Intimidation, or Performance; you gain a +2 item bonus to that skill. 

**Activate—Encore** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a bard composition spell. If you don't spend this point by the end of this turn, it is lost. 

**Activate—Transcribe** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You tuck a small roll of paper into the cincture. For the next 10 minutes or until you Dismiss the activation, any performance you make is recorded on the paper, and the paper expands as necessary to accommodate it. Depending on the type of performance, this might take the form of sheet music, a transcript, or a diagram of dance moves.

**Craft Requirements** You are a bard.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

---

The skill bonus is +3, and Transcribe lasts up to 1 hour.

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290 **Price** 1,250 gp **Usage** worn collar **Bulk** L --- This engraved duskwood gorget seems to vibrate with ferocity, granting you a +2 item bonus to Intimidation checks. **Activate—Primal Roar** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once during the duration of each polymorph effect; **Requirements** You're in a non-humanoid form via a polymorph effect; **Effect** You unleash a bestial roar, attempting a single Intimidation check compared to the Will DCs of all enemies within 30 feet to impose the effects below. **Critical Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) 2
**Success** The creature is frightened 1
**Failure** The creature is unaffected
name: Gorget of the Primal Roar

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Healer's Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3087)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These clean, white gloves never show signs of blood, even after they're used to stitch up wounds or treat other ailments. They give you a +1 item bonus to Medicine checks. 

**Activate—Healer's Touch** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You soothe the wounds of a willing, living, adjacent creature, restoring 2d6+7 Hit Points to that creature. This is a healing vitality effect. You can't harm undead with this healing. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290 **Usage** worn --- Fine silk lines this fashionable pocket, which is typically cinched to a belt or tailored into a piece of formal clothing. The pocket can hold no more than one item of light Bulk, plus incidental items of negligible Bulk. The pocket grants you a +2 item bonus to Society and to Stealth checks to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) in the pocket.
The pocket can produce stationery and writing implements of high quality. When you [Create a Forgery](/Actions.aspx?ID=60), you can use the pocket to produce ideal materials to make the forgery. Any materials must be able to fit through the opening of the pocket, such as a roll of parchment, an inkwell, or a loupe. Though they're excellent tools, they don't have any value if sold and disappear once their function in making a forgery is fulfilled. **Activate—Papers Please** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You create a temporary forgery by imagining the document you need and pulling it from the pocket. Attempt to [Create a Forgery](/Actions.aspx?ID=60) of the document you desire, with the GM rolling the secret check as normal. Its quality is based on your check, but the document disintegrates after 1 hour. Humbug Pocket **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290 **Price** 650 gp Humbug Pocket (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 290 **Price** 12,500 gp --- The item bonus is +3. You can forge up to five documents with Papers Please, but they must serve a similar purpose— five invitations to the same party for different attendees, for example. Attempt only one check for all the documents.
name: Humbug Pocket

item_bonus_note: Crafting checks to Craft, Earn Income, and Repair
markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 291 **Price** 19,000 gp **Usage** worn garment **Bulk** 2 --- This strange and intricate harness fits snugly to the torso. Once you invest the apparatus, numerous artificial limbs with various tools, clamps, and lenses whirl into action, following your mental commands effortlessly.
When using this apparatus, you gain a +3 item bonus to Crafting checks to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43), [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23), and [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42), and you reduce the minimum time required to Craft an item to 1 day. If you succeed at your Crafting check and spend more downtime to continue work on the item after the minimum number of days, each day you spend reduces the remaining raw material cost by an amount based on your level + 1 and your proficiency rank in Crafting; on a critical success, each day reduces the remaining raw material cost by an amount based on your level + 2 and your proficiency rank. If you are 20th level, on a critical success your progress is 50 gp, 100 gp, 200 gp, or 350 gp for trained, expert, master, or legendary proficiency, respectively. **Activate—Inexplicable Patch** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You command the apparatus to magically jury-rig an item you hold or that's within 5 feet of you. The item is repaired, as a 3rd-rank [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=189) spell. This lasts for 10 minutes, after which the item returns to its previous state of disrepair unless you've Repaired it before then.
name: Inexplicable Apparatus

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lifting Belt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3091)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wide leather belt grants you a +1 item bonus to Athletics checks and increases the amount you can easily carry. You can carry Bulk equal to 6 + your Strength modifier before becoming encumbered, and you can hold and carry a total Bulk up to 11 + your Strength modifier. 

**Activate—Assisted Lift** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You lift an object of up to 8 Bulk as though it were weightless. This requires two hands, and if the object is locked or otherwise held in place, you can attempt to Force it Open using Athletics as part of this activation. The object still has its full weight and Bulk for all other purposes—you just ignore that weight. The effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lifting Belt

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=676" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The base of this cloak is a thick layer of moss, but it slowly picks up native plants from each area it spends time in. You gain a +2 item bonus to Nature checks. You also suffer no effects from severe cold and severe heat. 

**Activate—Druidic Secrets** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast an order spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a druid.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lover's Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3093)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=590" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These white silk gloves are adorned in red hearts that glow faintly whenever you are adjacent to someone you feel particularly strongly toward. They buoy your spirit, giving you a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks. 

**Activate—Bond** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You grasp the hands of a willing creature you have strong positive feelings about, regardless of the nature of those feelings. The creature gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws and 10 temporary Hit Points for 10 minutes. If the creature shares your feelings, you gain the same benefits, and for the duration, when you both roll a success on a saving throw against an emotion effect that causes negative emotions, you both get a critical success instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lover's Gloves
