<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mage's Hat](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3094)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=534" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hat comes in many forms, such as a colorful turban or a pointy hat with a brim, and is adorned with symbols or runes. It grants you a +1 item bonus to Arcana checks and allows you to cast the [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=229) cantrip as an arcane innate cantrip.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Mage's Hat
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Mage's Hat (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This larger, fancier hat grants a +2 bonus and can be activated. Each greater mage's hat has a specific 4th-rank summon spell from the arcane list woven into its fabric, typically [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) or [_summon elemental_](/Spells.aspx?ID=320). If you prepare arcane spells, you can change the spell to a different 4th-rank arcane summon spell you know when you invest it. 

**Activate—Hat Spell** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You doff the hat, causing magical energy to pour from it. You cast the spell stored in the hat.
name: Mage's Hat

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 292

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This larger, fancier hat grants a +2 bonus and can be activated. Each greater mage's hat has a specific 4th-rank summon spell from the arcane list woven into its fabric, typically [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) or [_summon elemental_](/Spells.aspx?ID=320). If you prepare arcane spells, you can change the spell to a different 4th-rank arcane summon spell you know when you invest it. 

**Activate—Hat Spell** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You doff the hat, causing magical energy to pour from it. You cast the spell stored in the hat.
name: Mage's Hat (Greater)

---

This silver signet ring changes to match the insignia of a leader or organization you serve (or your own face, if you serve no one else). It grants you a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks and lets you cast [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) as an arcane innate spell at will. 

**Activate—Dispatch Messenger** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The ring casts [_animal messenger_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1441) to your specification. The animal is a magical creature that springs from the ring, and its appearance suits the iconography or heraldry of the leader or organization represented by the ring.

    pfs="Standard"

[Obsidian Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3097)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 293

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The sleek, black lenses of these goggles somehow make everything seem more brightly lit. While wearing the goggles, you gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight. 

**Activate—Darkvision** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Adjusting your goggles, you gain darkvision for 1 hour. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Obsidian Goggles
</title>

</traits>

</row>

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Obsidian Goggles (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 293

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, the goggles can be activated any number of times per day, and the activation lasts until you deactivate it as an Interact action or the item is no longer invested by you, whichever comes first.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Obsidian Goggles (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 293

**Price** 20,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +3, the goggles can be activated any number of times per day, the activation grants [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), and the activation lasts until you deactivate it as an Interact action or the item is no longer invested by you, whichever comes first.
name: Obsidian Goggles

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 293

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Despite covering the entire face, this alabaster mask does not hinder vision or other senses. Wearing the mask grants a +1 item bonus to Performance checks while acting, orating, performing comedy, or singing. 

**Activate—Assume Role** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))    **Effect** You change the mask's appearance into an artistic rendition of a dramatic character of your choice.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 3"

>
Persona Mask

</traits>

</row>

right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Persona Mask (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 293, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 613

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The mask grants a +2 bonus and gains another activation. 

**Activate—Sacrifice Role** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76))
 **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail a Performance check that benefits from the mask's bonus;  **Effect** You change the mask's character and reroll the Performance check, using the second result.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Primeval Mistletoe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3100)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sprig of berry-festooned holly and mistletoe doesn't wilt or rot. It can be used as a primal locus, and it grants a +1 item bonus to Nature checks while you wear it. 

**Activate—Anoint** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** You squeeze juice from one of the berries and smear it onto a weapon made primarily of wood to cast [_runic weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658) on it, or onto a creature to cast [_runic body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1657) on it. 

**Activate—Bind** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch the sprig, then a tree to cast [_one with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) upon yourself, turning into a vine on the touched tree.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Primeval Mistletoe
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 574

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Primeval Mistletoe (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 294, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 574

**Price** 3,900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The sprig grants a +2 item bonus to Nature checks, the spell from Anoint is heightened to 6th rank, and the sprig can be activated in one additional way:

**Activate—Cultivate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You plant the _greater primeval mistletoe_ into an area of natural earth or stone. Once planted, the plant immediately sprouts into an area of holly bushes that don't impede movement and that pulse with vitality energy, replicating the effects of a [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=114) spell. You can Sustain the activation up to 1 minute. When this magic ends, the holly bushes revert back into the original _greater primeval mistletoe_.
name: Primeval Mistletoe

</title>

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 295

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

If you worship a deity, this golden amulet transforms into your deity's religious symbol when you invest it. You gain a +1 item bonus to Religion. The symbol casts dim light in a 20-foot emanation. 

**Activate—Spiritual Light** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The light cast by the symbol becomes bright light for 10 minutes and shines through bodies to reveal hints of the spirits within. Creatures in the light receive a –1 status penalty to Deception and Stealth checks. You can Dismiss this activation.

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />

</row>

</column>

---

This tarnished necklace can be attuned only by someone who is holy or unholy. When you attune it, it transforms into your deity's religious symbol or a personal symbol if you don't have a deity. You receive a +1 item bonus to Religion and a +1 circumstance bonus to saves against holy and unholy effects. 

**Activate—Presence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The symbol casts [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=18) or [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25). The counteract DC of these effects is 18, and the counteract modifier is +8.

**Craft Requirements**  You must be holy or unholy.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Symbol of Conflict
</title>

</row>

</column>

The bonus to Religion is +2. Change the list of spells to 4th- rank [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=18), [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25), [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), and [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467). The DC is 27 and the counteract modifier is +17.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Symbol of Conflict (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 19,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

The bonus to Religion is +3. Change the list of spells to 6th- rank bane, bless, divine wrath, and cleanse affliction. Add 6th-rank [_summon celestial_](/Spells.aspx?ID=317) to this list if you are holy and 6th- rank [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=323) if you are unholy. The DC is 38 and the counteract modifier is +28.

**Activate—Presence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The symbol casts [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=18) or [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=25). The counteract DC of these effects is 18, and the counteract modifier is +8.

**Craft Requirements**  You must be holy or unholy.

    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Whisper of the First Lie](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3118)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 297

**Price** 60,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This delicate necklace contains bottled whispers distilled from a source on the Astral Plane rumored to be connected to the first lie ever told. While wearing the necklace, you gain a +3 item bonus to Deception checks, and you can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) effects that would force you to tell the truth or determine whether you are lying. Success on this counteract attempt lets you ignore the effect, rather than removing the effect entirely. The counteract rank is 9, with a counteract modifier of +35. 

**Activate—Release the Lie** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))    **Effect** You unstopper the vial and release the lie, creating the effect of a [_fabricated truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=106) (DC 47). The vial is emptied and can never be activated again.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of _fabricated truth_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Whisper of the First Lie

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 25"
    pfs=""
>
[Philosopher's Extractor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3122)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 301

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 8
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bizarre machine is a complex arrangement of flasks, tubes, and other alchemical equipment. The _philosopher's extractor_ is designed to create the ultimate alchemical concoctions. The extractor functions as an [exceptional alchemist's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2702), granting a +4 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) checks related to alchemy.  When using the extractor to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) an alchemical item or with [infused reagents](/Classes.aspx?ID=1) as part of your daily preparations, you can create impeccable alchemical items. An impeccable alchemical item always uses the maximum numerical value possible for any rolls it requires, such as dealing maximum damage with [alchemist's fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75) or restoring the maximum number of Hit Points with an [elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91). If the impeccable item has a duration, it lasts twice as long as normal. Finally, an impeccable alchemical item never has a drawback.

**Activate—Accelerated Synthesis** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You use the extractor to produce an alchemical item of your level or lower whose formula you know. The extractor can create 56 levels' worth of items per day in this way. For example, the extractor could create two true elixirs of life (19th level) and two moderate elixirs of life (9th level), or eight [comprehension elixirs](/Equipment.aspx?ID=88) (7th level), and so on.

**Activate—Essence Transmogrification** <actions string="" /> 1 hour (manipulate)    **Effect** You take a sizable portion of a creature (at least two-thirds of its original mass) and filter it through the mechanisms of the extractor. After the end of the process, the _philosopher's extractor_ creates a transmogrifying mutagen that imparts the essence of the creature to the drinker. The extractor can make several transmogrifying mutagens simultaneously using the same activation if enough mass is provided at once, up to a maximum of 10 concurrent mutagens. Drinking a transmogrifying mutagen imparts you with one of the creature's unique abilities for 1 hour. This could grant one of several abilities such as a dragon's breath, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), flight, [frightful presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17), or immunity to [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145). The ability functions as it did for the original creature, except it uses your class DC or your spell DC (whichever is higher) instead of the creature's DC. The mutagen grants only abilities based on a creature's physiology and never grants magic-related abilities such as innate spells or spellcasting ability. The GM ultimately decides what ability a transmogrifying mutagen grants.

 Unlike normal for [mutagens](/Traits.aspx?ID=116) and [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127) effects, you can drink multiple transmogrifying mutagens and gain benefits from each. When you drink transmogrifying mutagens made from different creatures, you receive abilities from each creature. When you drink multiple transmogrifying mutagens made from the same type of creature, such as multiple mutagens made from trolls, you gain an additional ability for each transmogrifying mutagen you drink, and the duration of the abilities from that creature type increases by 1 hour for each additional concoction you drink.

 However, these additional benefits come with a risk; if you drink a transmogrifying mutagen while at least one other is active, after drinking it, you must succeed at a flat check with a DC equal to the total number of active transmogrifying mutagens you've consumed. On a failure, you fully transform into a member of the species of the latest transmogrifying mutagen you drank, and you almost always go berserk from your change. The transformation may leave some amount of your personality and memories intact, at the GM's discretion.

**Destruction** Simultaneously feeding the _philosopher's extractor_ sizable portions of an [aeon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=2), [angel](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=3), [archon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=9), [azata](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=10), [daemon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=26), [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), [devil](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30), [protean](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=82), and [psychopomp](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83), each of at least 14th level, along with a [philosopher's stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=133) causes the device to jam, overheat, and explode. Creatures within 60 feet of the explosion must succeed at a DC 55 Reflex save or become covered in an alchemical slurry that transforms them into a horrifying amalgam of at least two of the creatures used to destroy the machine.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Philosopher's Extractor

---

This 6-foot-long scarf shimmers with the silvery light of the River of Souls while worn, providing illumination equivalent to that of a candle. You can deactivate or activate this radiance as an Interact action. The dangling lengths of a _ghost scarf_ softly billow in the presence of haunts, granting the wearer a +1 item bonus to all Perception checks and Perception DCs to resolve discovering a haunt or rolling initiative when a haunt triggers. 

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The scarf extends silvery threads that wrap around a weapon you carry, granting the effects of a _[ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840)_ property rune to that weapon for 5 minutes. If the weapon already bears a _ghost touch_ rune, you instead gain a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saves against effects from incorporeal undead for 5 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<traits>

</row>

</row>

</column>

---

The flower that adorns this hairpin is a blooming lotus flower that varies in color; it regrows in a day after the item is activated. Whether or not the hairpin's flower is in bloom, as long as you wear it and it's invested, it grants a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks. 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pluck the flower from the hairpin and scatter the petals, creating a flurry of color in a 10-foot burst centered on you. You become concealed for 1 minute or until you move from your current location. Any creature within the 10-foot burst when you Activate the Item must succeed at a DC 23 Reflex save or become [dazzled ](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of its next turn (or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn on a critical failure). The flower blooms again the next day.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

The soles of these plain leather boots look like coils of flattened roots. The boots allow for full-foot flexibility despite their sturdy make and smell faintly of evergreen trees. While worn and invested, your footsteps sound like rustling leaves, granting you a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks in a forest or wooded area. 

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The small roots extending from the soles of these boots allow you to move freely and easily across wood. For 10 minutes, you gain a 20-foot climb Speed on trees and other wooden surfaces, and you don't need to use your hands to climb. 

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're standing on earth, and something attempts to move you against your will;  **Effect** The roots extend from your boots to grip the ground, granting you a +4 item bonus to your Fortitude DC against effects that attempt to move you, such as Shove or Pull.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Covenant Tea](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3140)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Tea" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=738" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #197: Let the Leaves Fall](/Sources.aspx?ID=222) pg. 77

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact or 10 minutes (concentrate, Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This green tea is often steeped in a pot with a dried persimmon. Traditionally, all participants in a complex discussion start their conversation with a tea ceremony involving _covenant tea_, with each member of the group enjoying the covenant of the shared beverage. Some have been known to use trickery and sleight of hand to ensure only those whose goals align with the tea preparer are served _covenant tea_, while others in the group are served non-magical (but still delicious) green tea, thus subtly tipping the balance in discussion toward those the tea server favors. This practice has resulted in some regions referring to covenant tea as “trickery tea.” Regardless of what you prefer to call the tea, when you drink it, you gain a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks and to your Perception DC for 10 minutes. 

---

 [Tea Ceremony](/Traits.aspx?ID=738) The duration increases to 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Covenant Tea

---

This sweet and refreshing tea is made by adding a mixture of honey, lemon slices, and sliced ginger. This is considered to be the bare minimum when serving _energizing tea_, and many go above and beyond by adding additional citrus fruits or berries to the mix or refusing to serve the tea at all without accompanying it with a full platter of spiced pastries and sweets. This golden tea has energizing properties and, when consumed, grants you a +1 item bonus to Athletics and Acrobatics checks for 10 minutes.

_Spiritsight tea_ exudes a soft blue glow, creating illumination equal to that of a candle. When consumed, your eyes take on a soft glow as well, and you can see invisible creatures and objects for 10 minutes; such creatures appear to you as translucent shapes, and they're [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to you. You gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs=""
>
[Injigo's Loving Embrace](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3147)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #198: No Breath to Cry](/Sources.aspx?ID=224) pg. 77

**Price** 675 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This net was woven by the jorogumo Injigo, who presented it as a gift to her gnome sweetheart, Mara, to keep her safe in their mountain cottage when Injigo left on hunting expeditions.

 _Injigo's Loving Embrace_ functions as a typical [net](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2738). You gain a +1 item bonus to Athletics checks made to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) with the net.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You spin the net in the direction of an incorporeal creature within 30 feet that you can see. Lines of silken force whip out to lash around the incorporeal creature, who must attempt a DC 25 Reflex save to avoid becoming [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until the end of your next turn. You can Sustain this activation for up to 1 minute, and the creature must succeed at a DC 25 check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) the net.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You wrap the net around you like a blanket as you prepare to sleep. As long as you get 8 hours of sleep or analogous rest, you dream of writing love letters to someone you adore: this could be an actual paramour, someone you're interested in, or someone unknown whose identity you don't recall after waking. When you wake, you're filled with a sense of melancholia about those unsent letters, but these feelings distract you from other, more unpleasant thoughts, granting you resistance 5 to mental damage and a +1 item bonus to saving throws against mental effects for the next 8 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Injigo's Loving Embrace

    pfs=""
>
[Kinburi's Sandals of Bounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3148)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #198: No Breath to Cry](/Sources.aspx?ID=224) pg. 77

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These elevated wooden sandals once belonged to a haughty kitsune named Kinburi. It was well known that her swiftness and grace were unmatched, and observers described her as seeming to glide above the earth with each step. She often challenged others to wager on her speed, but as people began to grow wise, she was forced to conceal her identity or promise to forfeit her sandals if she lost.

 These sandals give you a +1 item bonus to Athletics checks to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378). In addition, when you use the [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) action, you can move 5 feet further if jumping horizontally or 3 feet higher if jumping vertically.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> (command);  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You loudly boast about your grace and agility, then take any combination of two High Jumps or Long Jumps. You do not need to Stride first, and between the two jumps, you need only to come into contact with a physical object before making the second jump; this object doesn't need to be one that can bear your weight, or even be attached to the ground—you can jump off from a floating feather or a thin branch or even off a paper wall as needed. The sandals' item bonus increases to +2 for these jumps.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Kinburi's Sandals of Bounding

    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Energizing Pill](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3152)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 73

**Price** 180 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This special blend of potent herbs comes in the form of a compact ball that allows it to be held in the mouth, where it remains for 1 hour. An energizing pill has a bitter taste at first, but the longer one lets it soak, the more complex and sweet its flavor grows. It heightens your reactions, granting you a +2 item bonus to initiative rolls with Perception.

 **Secondary Effect** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** You would become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94); **Effect** Reduce the value of the condition you just gained by 1; if this reduces the value to 0, you don't gain the triggering condition.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Energizing Pill

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Dragonfire Halfbow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3162)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 75

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in one hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafted by layering bamboo with strips of sliced scales harvested from a fire-breathing dragon, this bow has a significant draw strength. This design is commonly seen in Hongal, as the shorter limbs allow for easier maneuvering on horseback. While the wielder of this _+2 striking [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435)_ is mounted, they apply the bow's item bonus to Nature checks to Command your mount. 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw the bow back to its maximum draw length without nocking an arrow, and as you release the string, the _dragonfire halfbow_ casts [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) (heightened to 5th- level; DC 29 Reflex save), targeted at a point of your choosing within the spell's range. You and your mount are immune to the effects of this fireball.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragonfire Halfbow

    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Fan of Falling Words](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3168)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 76

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This folding handheld paper fan appears blank when first found, but if the wielder unfolds its leaves as an Interact action, they display an ink-brush painting of the wielder's choice. The fan must be closed and opened again to display a different painting. While held, the _fan of falling words_ grants you a +2 item bonus to Performance checks to act or perform comedy, orate, or sing. 

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You manifest the tapestry of your words, causing the image you depict on the fan to leap off its leaves. The fan casts [_hallucinatory terrain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=145) to your specifications (DC 27 Will save to disbelieve). While this only takes three actions to perform (as opposed to the spell's normal 10 minutes of casting), the effect has no range, and the 50-foot burst it creates is centered on you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fan of Falling Words

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 77 **Price** 1,300 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- In many parts of Tian Xia, it's tradition to mint coins with holes in the centers for the purpose of being able to thread a cord through them and tie them off for security and ease of transaction. This immaculately braided red cord is made in this tradition and can hold up to 100 coins at any given time. As long as you carry a _fortune cord_, you gain a +2 item bonus to skill checks to Earn Income. If you load up to 100 coins onto a _fortune cord_ as part of your daily preparations, you can Activate the magic item in two different ways until the next time you make your daily preparations, provided all the loaded coins are of the same type. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** you have at least 50 coins loaded onto the _fortune cord_; **Effect** You transform the fortune cord into a _+2 striking [nunchaku](/Weapons.aspx?ID=414)_ for 1 minute. The type of coin loaded onto the cord determines the additional weapon property rune that manifests on this _+2 striking [nunchaku](/Weapons.aspx?ID=414)_, as follows.
  • **Copper**: _[corrosive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2834) weapon_
  • **Silver**: _[shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847) weapon_
  • **Gold**: _[flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838) weapon_
  • **Platinum**: _[frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839) weapon_
**Activate** manipulate, Interact; **Effect** You whip the fortune cord, causing 10 coins loaded onto it to detach and fire at a creature within 100 feet that you can see. That creature takes 4d6 damage (DC 28 basic Reflex save); the damage type depends on the type of coins you've loaded. Copper coins deal acid damage, silver coins deal electricity damage, gold coins deal fire damage, and platinum coins deal cold damage.
name: Fortune Cord

item_bonus_note: All skill checks made to adjust a psychopomp’s or spirit’s attitude.
markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 77 **Price** 1,820 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12) --- This ash wood staff is topped by a long tassel of bleached-white horsehair. Historically carried by the most important of court officials throughout various regions and periods of Tian Xia's history, purgatory emissary's staves are also strongly associated with psychopomps serving punitive sentences. It's thought this connection is a comical nod to the bureaucratic nature of the afterlife. Psychopomps and spirits tend to regard you with a level of respect while you carry a _purgatory emissary's staff_. While you wield this staff, you receive a +2 item bonus to all skill checks made to adjust a psychopomp's or spirit's attitude. When used as a weapon, a purgatory emissary's staff is a _+2 striking [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.
  • **Cantrip** [_disrupt undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=79)
  • **1st** [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660)
  • **2nd** [_remove fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=252), [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271)
  • **3rd** [_searing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=269), [_zone of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=379)
  • **4th** [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712)
  • **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)
name: Purgatory Emissary's Staff

</title>

<trait label="Graft" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=790" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** implanted

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Special cells in your skin can change color to help you blend in with your environment. You gain the listed item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) and [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404).

**Activate—Background Adaptation** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 1 minute, you can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) without needing [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) or [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to do so. This doesn’t allow you to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) without ending your movement in cover or concealment, however, as your skin’s attempts to match the background as you move produce noticeable rippling waves of color.

You have a strong, prehensile tail. You gain a tail [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attack that deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage. This tail is in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) and to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374). You can also use your tail for the [Grab an Edge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2313) action, even if your hands are otherwise occupied.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hippopotamus Klar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3200)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 100

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Klar](/Shields.aspx?ID=12)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [klar](/Shields.aspx?ID=12)_ is fashioned from the skull of a hippopotamus, with one of its canines acting as the blade. While wielding a _hippopotamus klar_, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks.

**Activate—Engulfing Jaws** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The hippo jaws of the shield open impossibly wide. Make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with the klar blade targeting two creatures adjacent to each other. Roll the attack and damage once and apply it to each creature separately. This counts as two attacks for your [multiple attack penalty](/Rules.aspx?ID=2289).

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the entire skull of a [hippopotamus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=699).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hippopotamus Klar

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Arachnolute](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3220)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 105

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The strings of this spider-shaped lute are made from the webbing of a [goliath spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3209) and the tuning pegs are crafted from the spider’s spinnerets. An _arachnolute_ grants you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument.

**Activate—Web Chord** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Sticky webbing sprays in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) as you strum the lute’s strings. Each creature in the area of the webbing is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) unless it succeeds at a DC 29 Reflex save.

**Craft Requirements** The initial materials must include the spinnerets of a [goliath spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3209), as well as a spool of its webbing.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Arachnolute

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 105 **Price** 2,600 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 2 --- The main portion of this set of bagpipes is fashioned from the dried skin of a [cauthooj](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2870), with the feathers still attached. The bird’s vocal cords are crafted into the instrument’s reeds. The pipes grant a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with them. **Activate—Disorienting Fugue** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You play several notes on the pipes, quickly altering their pitch and tone. Each creature within a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 31 Will save to resist the song. Creatures that attempt this save become temporarily immune to Disorienting Fugue for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected and its temporary immunity to Disorienting Fugue increases to 1 hour.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is confused for 1 round and immediately attacks itself (in the normal fashion for attacking oneself while confused). This [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) doesn’t give the creature a flat check to recover from the confusion. **Activate—Throw Song** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet attempts a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against you or an ally; **Effect** You let loose a staccato chirp from the pipes that appears to come from somewhere else. The triggering creature must attempt a DC 31 Will save. On a failure, the creature redirects the Strike to another creature of your choice within reach of the melee Strike. If no other creatures are within range, the affected creature instead takes a –2 penalty to the Strike. **Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the skin and vocal cords of a [cauthooj](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2870).
name: Cauthooj Bagpipes

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Amulet of the Hellcat](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3224)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 106

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glass amulet is set on a steel backing shaped like a cage and flickers with an eerie crimson glow. Magically sealed within is a portion of a hellcat's ever-burning heart. While wearing the amulet, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks made while in [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407), and you can cast the [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) cantrip emanating from the amulet as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2232) divine spell.

**Activate—Invisible Pounce** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are in bright light; **Effect** You become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to your speed, and then make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) at the end of that movement. After making the Strike, you are no longer invisible. If at any point during your Stride, you pass out of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402), you are no longer invisible and must stop moving. You may make a melee Strike if you have a target within reach.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the heart of a [hellcat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=695).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Amulet of the Hellcat

This long hide cloak is stitched with patches of the ebony hair of a [tikbalang](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1338). While wearing this cloak, you take a –2 item penalty to saves against illusions but gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks.

**Activate—Illusory Thrash** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You wrap the mantle around your body, causing you to briefly appear much larger than you are. Make a melee Strike. This Strike deals an additional 4d6 mental damage.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hide of a [tikbalang](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1338).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 109

</row>

---

This oily mix of herbs and natural detergents can be applied to a creature to reduce and cover any ordinary odors they produce. The creature receives a +2 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) against creatures using primarily smell. This bonus also applies to the DC to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) the creature by scent. The listed amount is enough to cover one Medium or smaller creature and takes 1 minute to apply. The effect lasts for 1 hour after applying.

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />

</row>

---

You sprout a second head, increasing your awareness, intuition, and cognitive ability but also causing your physical capabilities to be impaired as both minds struggle to control a single body.

**Benefit** For 10 minutes, you gain [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) and a +3 item bonus to all Intelligence- and Wisdom-based skill checks. Once during the mutagen's effect, if you fail or critically fail a Will saving throw against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect, you can treat the result as one degree of success better.

**Drawback** You are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Your eyes bulge from their sockets and extend upwards from your head on long, prehensile stalks, greatly enhancing your eyesight and field of view but leaving you unable to close your eyes, increasing your vulnerability to harmful visual effects.

**Benefit** You gain [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49), a +2 item bonus to visual [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks, and [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411) if you don’t already have it. These effects last for 1 minute.

**Drawback** For 1 minute, you take a –2 penalty to saving throws against effects that have the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait.

Animal calls are often whistles or similar devices that imitate the calls of [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531). Each call is for a specific type of animal, such as a duck or bear. When you use the call, it gives you a +1 item bonus to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400), provided the animal is of the type as the call. You also do not take a circumstance penalty when attempting to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) that animal for not sharing a language.

---

This sturdy pole has a rope attached to one end in a loop with the other end extending to the handle. You can pull the handle side of the rope to tighten the loop. Using this loop, you can [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) without having a free hand. A creature grappled this way receives a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls when [Striking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with an unarmed attack. Due to limitations in the size of the loop, a catch pole can only be used on creatures sized Medium or smaller.

---

Made with a brightly colored leather strap, these goggles have a series of overlapping lenses in an array of colors spanning the spectrum of visible light. The effect of this is that every color is more vibrant and similar shades have higher contrast. This means that shapes of even similar colors tend to stand out more, allowing the wearer to see even camouflage easier.

As an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action, you can set the lenses to make things stand out at your current location. Until you move, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks when you [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: High-Contrast Goggles

The plates are arranged for maximum flexibility and the undercoat of padded armor is often no more than a cotton shrift. A _glamorous [buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=17)_ is lavishly decorated with gilding and inset gemstones that glitter in the light. While you have it raised, the _glamorous buckler_ grants you a +1 item bonus to Deception checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390).

**Activate—Dazzling Feint** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390); **Requirements** You have the glamorous buckler raised; **Effect** As you Feint, the _glamorous buckler_ sparkles mightily. On a successful Feint, the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.

</column>

Your features transform into something bestial and lumbering. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and unarmed attack rolls. You gain a claw unarmed attack with the agile trait and a jaws unarmed attack. Striking runes don't modify the damage caused by these attacks. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to Reflex saves, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks.

</row>

---

Your mind becomes clear, but physical matters seem ephemeral. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Arcana, Crafting, Lore, Occultism, and Society checks and all checks to Recall Knowledge. Your critical failures on [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks become failures instead. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to weapon and unarmed attack rolls, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, and [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks. You can carry 2 less Bulk than normal before becoming [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70), and the maximum Bulk you can carry is reduced by 4.

Your mind becomes clear, but physical matters seem ephemeral. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Arcana, Crafting, Lore, Occultism, and Society checks and all checks to Recall Knowledge. Your critical failures on [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks become failures instead. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to weapon and unarmed attack rolls, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, and [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks. Your mind becomes clear, but physical matters seem ephemeral. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Arcana, Crafting, Lore, Occultism, and Society checks and all checks to Recall Knowledge. Your critical failures on [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks become failures instead. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to weapon and unarmed attack rolls, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, and [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cognitive Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3316)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=808" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your mind becomes clear, but physical matters seem ephemeral. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Arcana, Crafting, Lore, Occultism, and Society checks and all checks to Recall Knowledge. Your critical failures on [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks become failures instead. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to weapon and unarmed attack rolls, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, and [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks. You can carry 2 less Bulk than normal before becoming [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70), and the maximum Bulk you can carry is reduced by 4.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 547

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cognitive Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cognitive Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 289, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 547

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3, and the duration is 1 hour. You become trained in one Intelligence-based skill, chosen at creation.

<title

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Quicksilver Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3319)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=808" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You become swifter and nimbler but also fragile. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks, Reflex saves, and Dexterity-based attack rolls, and you gain the listed status bonus to your Speed. 

**Drawback** You take damage equal to twice your level; you can't recover Hit Points lost in this way by any means while the mutagen lasts. You take a –2 penalty to Fortitude saves.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Quicksilver Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +1, the bonus to Speed is +5 feet, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Quicksilver Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +2, the bonus to Speed is +10 feet, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Quicksilver Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

**Drawback** You take damage equal to twice your level; you can't recover Hit Points lost in this way by any means while the mutagen lasts. You take a –2 penalty to Fortitude saves.
